name: Kam Transportation Entps
districts_or_customers: Howard County Public School System (HCPSS) contractor
recent_news: Small Glenelg, MD HCPSS contractor; listed in HCPSS school bus contractor directory. No recent M&A or growth news found.
fit_notes: Sub-scale single-route HCPSS contractor (~1-3 buses, ~$60-240K revenue). Too small to be a meaningful Summit tuck-in on its own; only of interest as part of a roll-up of multiple Howard County micro-contractors.

name: Kang Transportation
districts_or_customers: Likely Baltimore City Public Schools area (unconfirmed)
recent_news: No web presence, no website, no news, not found on Baltimore City/County approved contractor lists in available search results. Possible owner-operator at residential address (5130 Linden Heights Ave).
fit_notes: Cannot confirm this is an active independent school-bus operator versus an inactive/owner-operator entity. Too small and opaque to be a Summit tuck-in target without further phone verification.

name: Bay City School Transportation
owner_or_parent: Bay City Public Schools (in-house district transportation department)
recent_news: District-run operation. In 2022 the district explored privatizing transportation by soliciting proposals from private companies, but as of 2024 it continued operating its in-house fleet of ~40 buses (annual cost ~$3M). Rolled out new bus tracking app in Aug 2024.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target -- this is the in-house transportation department of Bay City Public Schools (480 Midland Rd is the district's transportation facility), not an independent private contractor. However, it is a privatization-prospect customer: the district publicly considered outsourcing in 2022 and would be a business-development target, not an acquisition target.

name: J & R Bus Svc
districts_or_customers: Anne Arundel County area (Glen Burnie / Millersville); likely AACPS contractor
recent_news: Small owner-operator school bus service in Glen Burnie, MD. Maintains a Yelp listing and is referenced as serving the Millersville/Glen Burnie area. No M&A or expansion news found.
fit_notes: Sub-scale single-route Anne Arundel County contractor (~1-5 buses, ~$220K revenue). Too small individually for a Summit tuck-in; only relevant in a multi-contractor Anne Arundel roll-up.

name: Cassidy Transportation Inc
principals: Lori Cassidy (current owner, daughter of founder); John (son-in-law, co-owner); Sylvia Cassidy (founder, 1935-2020)
districts_or_customers: Educational Services Commission of Morris County
Warren County Special Service School District
Serves 700 students across 100+ schools in 10 NJ counties: Bergen, Essex, Hunterdon, Mercer, Middlesex, Morris, Passaic, Somerset, Sussex, Warren
recent_news: Family-owned, second-generation (founder Sylvia Cassidy passed away in 2020; daughter Lori and son-in-law John now run the business). NJSBCA member. No acquisition or sale news identified.
fit_notes: STRONG SUMMIT TUCK-IN CANDIDATE. ~130-vehicle special-needs/SPED-focused operator across 10 North/Central NJ counties with contracts at three county special-services cooperatives -- exactly the niche, high-margin SPED book Summit values. Second-generation family ownership (founder deceased 2020) creates a credible succession/exit narrative. NJ is a core Summit state. Headquarters Budd Lake, NJ (HQ address listed in 'Long Valley/Budd Lake'; phone area code 908 reflects same operating footprint as 973-area-code main line).
